name: PepsiCo
cat: Food & Beverage / Multinational Food and Beverage Corporation

political.s: <cite index="1-1,1-3">PepsiCo has identified forced labor and poor employment conditions as salient risks in high-risk commodities like cane sugar and palm oil in Latin America and Southeast Asia</cite>, indicating governance concerns. The company's bipartisan political engagement aligns with its stated commitment to contributing to society while maintaining competitive neutrality in political discourse.

charity.s: <cite index="12-1">The PepsiCo Foundation creates sustainable solutions in food security, safe water, and economic opportunity to drive positive impact in communities worldwide</cite>. <cite index="11-6">The company seeks to empower associates with funding and programming including scholarships and global workforce volunteering programs</cite>.
charity.focus: Food security, safe water access, economic opportunity, community development

environment.s: <cite index="33-6">PepsiCo extended its long-term net-zero commitment deadline from 2040 to 2050</cite>, reflecting adjustments to environmental goals. However, <cite index="35-5,35-6">the company reduced Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 13% and virgin plastic use from non-renewable sources by 4% per year</cite>, showing mixed progress on sustainability metrics.

labor.s: <cite index="1-1,1-3,1-5">PepsiCo's Supplier Code of Conduct has identified forced labor and poor employment conditions risks, but the company has provided investors no evidence of oversight or remediation across franchisees and high-risk commodities</cite>. <cite index="76-1">PepsiCo-owned companies have accumulated $68.6 million in regulatory penalties since 2000 across 230 violation records</cite>.

dei.s: <cite index="21-1">PepsiCo is restructuring its approach to DEI, phasing out its Global Chief DEI Officer role as part of a new 'Inclusion for Growth' strategy</cite>. <cite index="24-5">The company CEO announced it will no longer set goals for minority representation in managerial roles or supplier base</cite>.

animals.s: <cite index="64-1">After hearing from PETA, PepsiCo announced it will not conduct or fund experiments on animals and pledged to communicate its opposition to animal testing</cite>. <cite index="66-1">PepsiCo does not conduct animal tests on food and beverage products except when required by law</cite>.

guns.s: PepsiCo is a food and beverage company with no involvement in firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: <cite index="52-1,52-2,52-3">PepsiCo's Privacy Principles promote consistency in data collection practices based on globally accepted standards and the company complies with privacy laws including CCPA and GDPR</cite>. <cite index="52-7">PepsiCo has a dedicated Privacy Office with privacy professionals operating globally</cite>.

execPay.s: <cite index="49-1">In 2024, Ramon Laguarta's pay was 538 times the median employee salary at PepsiCo, approximately $53,551</cite>. <cite index="44-1">Laguarta received $28.8 million in total compensation for 2024, a 15% decrease from 2023</cite>.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
